content.problemStatement:
Bubble Sort is a simple sorting algorithm that works by repeatedly swapping the adjacent elements if they are in the wrong order.

The algorithm works as:


Iterate over the array and check if any adjacent values are placed in the wrong order. If it is, swap these values.
Since after this iteration it would be guaranteed that the largest element would bubble up to the end of the array, if we apply this operation repeatedly, eventually the array will be sorted in the required order.
TaskBubble sort is implemented in the IDE, but is incomplete. 

Complete it to solve the problem.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer n (the number of elements). The second line contains n space‑separated integers representing the array.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output the sorted array in ascending order on its own line. Elements are separated by a single space.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The array is in descending order; bubble sort repeatedly swaps adjacent out‑of‑order pairs, moving the largest element to the end each pass. After all passes the array becomes sorted ascending.
The array is already sorted, so no swaps occur and the output remains unchanged.
Bubble sort transforms the descending array into ascending order through successive passes.

solution.approach: For each test case we apply the classic Bubble Sort algorithm: repeatedly scan the array, swapping any adjacent pair that is out of order. After the i‑th pass the largest i elements are guaranteed to be at the end, so the inner loop can stop earlier (n‑i‑1). The algorithm runs in O(n^2) time and O(1) extra space, which is acceptable for n ≤ 1000.
solution.timeComplexity: O(n^2) per test case, where n is the size of the array.
